summary: `drafts get` must resolve the draft via /v3/userdata.getThreads (matching thread id or message id), not the single-id userdata.read path — real Superhuman drafts have a distinct thread id and message id.
reason: readDraft built users/<uid>/threads/<id>/messages/<id>/draft reusing one id for both segments, so it never resolved: live drafts have thread id != message id (e.g. threads/draft007cf1…/messages/draft00f93…). getThreads returns the full draftValue per message, so drafts get now resolves through it. Note getThreads caps limit at 100 (>100 -> HTTP 400).
validated_outcome: Live against a real account: released binary -> 'not found' for an existing draft; patched -> resolves by both thread id and message id, returning subject/from/body. Tests rewritten to mock getThreads (thread-id != message-id shape).
